body.text: Four gun-rights groups ask the Sixth Circuit to strike the NFA's SBR registry — the litigation map quietly widens

NRA, FPC, SAF and the American Suppressor Association filed a July 13 amicus in U.S. v. Machamer: 1.18M registered SBRs are protected arms and the 1934 barrel-length line was a "historical accident." Same day: Maryland defended its Glock ban and Delaware moved to keep the under-21 case frozen.

facts.title: 1. Virginia — six days to the July 21 statewide injunction
2. SCOTUS AR-15 case (Viramontes/Grant) — Jul 20 order list
3. Barnett v. Raoul — cools into reference status

body.text:
• NEW: NRA+FPC+SAF+ASA amicus — NFA SBR registry fails Bruen (single-source)
• NEW: Maryland answers in Bathras — Glocks "capable of being fully automatic"
• NEW: Delaware moves to keep Birney under-21 challenge stayed
• MOVED: GOA opposes ATF EITB replacement rule; comments close Aug 4
